--- Page 2 ---
HR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal UseHR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal Use
1. Purpose
The purpose of this HR Chat Policy is to define the rules, responsibilities, privacy expectations, and usage 
standards for communication through the HR chat system, HR chatbot, or HR helpdesk chat channel. This 
policy helps ensure that HR-related conversations are professional, confidential, accurate, and compliant 
with company st

elpdesk chat channel. This 
policy helps ensure that HR-related conversations are professional, confidential, accurate, and compliant 
with company standards.
2. Scope
This policy applies to all users who access or interact with the HR chat system, including employees, 
interns, contractors, HR staff, managers, administrators, and any authorized third-party support teams.
 HR chatbot conversations and automated HR responses
 Manual HR helpdesk chat conversations
 Queries related to leave, attendance, payroll, benefits, policies, onboarding, documents, and employee 
support
 Any HR-related data shared through chat, attachments, forms, or integrated systems
3. Definitions
Term Meaning
HR Chat System A digital chat channel, chatbot, or helpdesk tool used 
for HR-related communication.
User Any person who accesses or communicates through 
the HR chat system.
Sensitive HR Data Personal, employment, payroll, health, identity, 
grievance, disciplinary, or confidential employee 
informatio

the HR chat system.
Sensitive HR Data Personal, employment, payroll, health, identity, 
grievance, disciplinary, or confidential employee 
information.
Bot Response An automated response generated by the HR chatbot 
or AI assistant.
Escalation Transfer of a chat query to an HR representative, 
manager, admin, or authorized support person.
4. Policy Statement
The HR chat system must be used only for legitimate HR and employee-support purposes. All users must 
maintain respectful communication, protect confidential information, and avoid misuse of the system. The 
chat system is intended to provide quick HR assistance, but it does not replace official HR decisions, signed 
company documents, legal notices, or management approvals.
5. Acceptable Use
Users may use the HR chat system for the following purposes:
 Asking questions about company HR policies, leave rules, attendance, payroll dates, benefits, 
onboarding, exit process, and employee documents.
 Submitting HR service requests,

y HR policies, leave rules, attendance, payroll dates, benefits, 
onboarding, exit process, and employee documents.
 Submitting HR service requests, such as leave clarification, profile update requests, payslip support, ID 
card support, and document requests.
 Receiving guidance about HR procedures and next steps.
 Checking status updates where the system supports status tracking.
 Requesting escalation to an HR representative when the chatbot answer is unclear or insufficient.

--- Page 3 ---
HR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal UseHR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal Use
6. Prohibited Use
Users must not use the HR chat system for any of the following:
 Sharing passwords, OTPs, private keys, or login credentials.
 Uploading or requesting unauthorized access to another employee’s personal or HR records.
 Using abusive, threatening, discriminatory, harassing, or unprofessional language.
 Making false claims, impersonating another person, or submitting misleading information

ning, discriminatory, harassing, or unprofessional lang uage.
 Making false claims, impersonating another person, or submitting misleading information.
 Attempting to manipulate, reverse-engineer, overload, or bypass system restrictions.
 Sharing confidential company data that is not required for the HR request.
 Using the chat system for non-HR, personal, political, promotional, or unrelated activities.
7. Privacy and Confidentiality
HR chat conversations may contain confidential and personal information. The organization must handle this 
information with appropriate care and restrict access to authorized personnel only.
 Only authorized HR staff, system administrators, or approved support personnel may access HR chat 
records when required for support, compliance, investigation, or audit purposes.
 Users should share only the minimum information required to resolve their HR query.
 Sensitive HR data must not be disclosed in group chats, public channels, or unsecured platforms.

mum information required to resolve their HR query.
 Sensitive HR data must not be disclosed in group chats, public channels, or unsecured platforms.
 The HR team must not share employee information with unauthorized persons.
 Any accidental disclosure of sensitive information must be reported immediately to HR or the designated 
security contact.
8. Data Collection and Retention
The HR chat system may collect and store chat messages, timestamps, user identifiers, uploaded 
attachments, query categories, chatbot responses, escalation notes, and resolution status. Retention 
periods should follow company policy and applicable legal requirements.
Data Type Purpose Suggested Retention
Chat transcripts Support history, quality review, 
audit, dispute resolution
6-24 months or as per company 
policy
Attachments Verification of employee requests As required for the request or legal 
retention
System logs Security monitoring and 
troubleshooting
90-365 days
Feedback ratings Service improve

ests As required for the request or legal 
retention
System logs Security monitoring and 
troubleshooting
90-365 days
Feedback ratings Service improvement 6-12 months
9. AI Chatbot Response Limitations
If an AI chatbot is used, users must understand that automated responses may be based on available 
company documents, configured rules, and system data. The chatbot may not always understand complex, 
outdated, missing, or exceptional cases.
 Bot responses are for guidance and support only unless clearly marked as official approval.
 Final decisions related to payroll, disciplinary action, hiring, termination, legal matters, and policy 
exceptions must be handled by authorized HR or management personnel.
 If a response seems incorrect, unclear, incomplete, or sensitive, the user should request escalation to 
HR.
 The chatbot must clearly indicate when it cannot answer and must provide an escalation option where 
possible.

--- Page 4 ---
HR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal UseHR

ly indicate when it cannot answer and must provide an escalation option where 
possible.

--- Page 4 ---
HR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal UseHR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal Use
10. Escalation Rules
The HR chat system must support escalation to a human HR representative in the following cases:
 Payroll errors, salary deductions, tax issues, or benefits disputes
 Harassment, discrimination, grievance, disciplinary, or workplace safety concerns
 Medical, legal, personal emergency, or sensitive employee issues
 Requests involving confidential documents or identity verification
 Cases where the chatbot cannot find a reliable answer
 Cases where the employee asks to speak with HR directly
11. Security Requirements
The organization should maintain reasonable security controls for the HR chat system.
 User authentication should be required before accessing personal HR data.
 Role-based access control should restrict data access based on job responsibilities.
 Sensitive da

d be required before accessing personal HR data.
 Role-based access control should restrict data access based on job responsibilities.
 Sensitive data should be encrypted in transit and, where applicable, at rest.
 Audit logs should record access to sensitive HR information.
 System administrators must not access chat data unless there is a valid support, security, compliance, 
or audit reason.
 Integrations with payroll, attendance, ERP, or document systems must use secure APIs and approved 
credentials.
12. Communication Standards
All communication through the HR chat system must be professional and respectful.
 Use clear and polite language.
 Avoid offensive, threatening, or discriminatory content.
 Do not use slang or unclear abbreviations for official HR requests.
 Provide accurate details when raising a request.
 Do not repeatedly submit duplicate requests unless the issue remains unresolved after the expected 
response time.
13. Monitoring and Quality Review
The compan

peatedly submit duplicate requests unless the issue remains unresolved after the expected 
response time.
13. Monitoring and Quality Review
The company may monitor HR chat activity for service quality, security, compliance, troubleshooting, 
training, and audit purposes. Monitoring must be limited to authorized purposes and should respect 
employee privacy.
14. Employee Rights and Responsibilities
Employee Rights
 To receive respectful and timely HR support.
 To request escalation to a human HR representative for sensitive or unclear issues.
 To request correction of inaccurate personal information through the proper HR process.
 To know that HR chat data may be stored and reviewed for legitimate business reasons.

--- Page 5 ---
HR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal UseHR Chat Policy | Confidential Internal Use
Employee Responsibilities
 Use the HR chat system only for HR-related purposes.
 Share accurate and necessary information only.
 Protect confidential information and av

ties
 Use the HR chat system only for HR-related purposes.
 Share accurate and necessary information only.
 Protect confidential information and avoid sharing another employee’s data.
 Report suspected misuse, privacy incidents, or incorrect chatbot responses.
15. HR Team Responsibilities
 Maintain accurate HR knowledge base content and update outdated policies promptly.
 Handle employee queries fairly, confidentially, and professionally.
 Review escalated conversations and provide appropriate resolutions.
 Ensure chatbot responses do not conflict with approved HR policies.
 Train HR users on privacy, confidentiality, and escalation procedures.
16. Incident Reporting
Any suspected misuse, data leak, unauthorized access, incorrect sensitive response, harassment, or policy 
violation must be reported immediately to HR, the reporting manager, or the designated security/contact 
person. The organization should investigate incidents based on severity and take appropriate corrective

g manager, or the designated security/contact 
person. The organization should investigate incidents based on severity and take appropriate corrective 
action.
17. Disciplinary Action
Violation of this policy may result in corrective or disciplinary action according to company policy. Serious 
violations may lead to access restriction, formal warning, termination of employment or contract, and legal 
action where applicable.
18. Policy Review and Updates
This policy should be reviewed at least every six months or whenever there are changes in HR processes, 
company policies, chatbot functionality, data protection requirements, or legal obligations. Updated versions 
must be communicated to all relevant users.
19. Standard HR Chat Disclaimer
The following disclaimer may be displayed at the beginning of the HR chat experience:
“This HR chat system provides general HR support and guidance based on available company 
information. Do not share passwords, OTPs, or unnecessary sensitive data.

system provides general HR support and guidance based on available company 
information. Do not share passwords, OTPs, or unnecessary sensitive data. Some conversations 
may be stored and reviewed by authorized personnel for support, quality, compliance, and security 
purposes. For confidential, legal, payroll, grievance, or emergency matters, please request 
escalation to HR.”
20. Acknowledgement
By using the HR chat system, users acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to follow this 
HR Chat Policy.

 Employee Travel and Expense Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy describes travel approvals, booking rules, reimbursement requirements, meal limits, and
expense claim timelines.
Employees must use reasonable judgment and choose cost-effective travel options for business trips.
Travel Approval
Domestic travel must be approved by the reporting manager before booking.
International travel must be approved by the department head and finance team before tickets are
purchased.
Emergency travel may be approved through email confirmation and regularized after the trip.
Booking Rules
Economy class must be used for domestic flights. Premium travel requires written department-head
approval.
Hotel bookings should follow the approved city-wise budget limits unless the client or event location

avel requires written department-head
approval.
Hotel bookings should follow the approved city-wise budget limits unless the client or event location
requires an exception.
Reimbursement
Expense claims must be submitted within ten working days after travel completion.
Original bills or digital receipts are required for hotel, flight, taxi, and meal expenses.
Claims without receipts may be rejected unless finance approves a documented exception.
Expense Type
Required Proof
Flight or Train
Ticket and invoice
Hotel
Tax invoice and payment receipt
Taxi or Rideshare
Trip receipt
Meals
Bill with date and location
Meal and Local Transport Limits
Meal reimbursement is allowed for business travel days only and must follow the company expense
limits.
Local transport should use standard taxi, rideshare, bus, metro, or approved rental services.

 Product Warranty and Returns Guide
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This guide explains product warranty coverage, return eligibility, replacement conditions, refund
timelines, and customer support process.
Warranty and returns are handled according to product category, purchase proof, issue type, and
usage condition.
Warranty Coverage
Standard electronic products include a one-year limited warranty from the date of purchase.
Accessories such as cables, chargers, and adapters include a six-month limited warranty unless
otherwise mentioned on the invoice.
Warranty covers manufacturing defects, power failure caused by internal faults, and non-physical
component malfunction.
Product Category
Warranty Period
Electronics
One year limited warranty
Accessories
Six months limited warranty
Replacement Un

l
component malfunction.
Product Category
Warranty Period
Electronics
One year limited warranty
Accessories
Six months limited warranty
Replacement Unit
Remaining original warranty or ninety days, whichever is longer
Return Eligibility
Customers may request a return within seven calendar days of delivery if the product is unused,
undamaged, and includes original packaging.
Opened products may be eligible for replacement if a verified manufacturing defect is found during
inspection.
Refund Timeline
Approved refunds are initiated within five to seven business days after the returned product passes
inspection.
Refunds are sent to the original payment method. Bank processing time may vary by payment
provider.
Warranty Exclusions
Warranty does not cover physical damage, water damage, unauthorized repair, misuse, normal wear
and tear, or missing serial numbers.
Products without valid purchase proof may be rejected for warranty service.

g serial numbers.
Products without valid purchase proof may be rejected for warranty service.

 IT Security and Data Protection Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy defines security practices for account access, passwords, devices, data handling, and
incident reporting.
All employees and contractors must protect company data and follow approved security controls.
Password Rules
Passwords must be at least twelve characters long and must not be reused across personal and
company accounts.
Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for email, code repositories, cloud platforms, and production
systems.
Device Security
Company laptops must use screen lock, disk encryption, antivirus protection, and operating system
updates.
Lost or stolen devices must be reported to IT within one hour of discovery.
Data Classification
Public data can be shared externally without restriction. Intern

olen devices must be reported to IT within one hour of discovery.
Data Classification
Public data can be shared externally without restriction. Internal data is limited to employees and
approved contractors.
Confidential data includes customer information, employee records, contracts, API keys, credentials,
and financial reports.
Restricted data must not be copied to personal devices, personal email, public drives, or unapproved
AI tools.
Classification
Examples
Public
Marketing brochures, public website text
Internal
Team notes, internal process documents
Confidential
Customer records, contracts, API keys
Restricted
Production secrets, regulated personal data
Incident Reporting
Suspected phishing, unauthorized access, malware, or accidental data exposure must be reported
immediately to the IT security team.
Employees should not delete evidence or attempt private investigation before reporting a security
incident.

idence or attempt private investigation before reporting a security
incident.

 HR Leave and Attendance Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy explains working hours, attendance expectations, leave categories, approval rules, and
documentation requirements for employees.
Employees are expected to record attendance accurately every working day. Missing attendance
records must be regularized within three working days.
Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM from Monday to Friday. A flexible grace period of 15
minutes is allowed up to three times in a month.
Repeated late login beyond the grace limit may be marked as half-day leave after manager review.
Leave Types
Casual leave can be used for short personal needs and should be requested at least one day in
advance whenever possible.
Sick leave can be taken for health reasons. If sick leave exc

rt personal needs and should be requested at least one day in
advance whenever possible.
Sick leave can be taken for health reasons. If sick leave exceeds two consecutive working days, a
medical certificate may be requested.
Earned leave must be planned and approved at least seven days before the start date unless an
exception is approved by HR.
Leave Type
Summary
Casual Leave
Short personal needs; advance notice preferred.
Sick Leave
Health reasons; certificate may be needed after two days.
Earned Leave
Planned leave; seven-day advance approval recommended.
Approval Rules
All leave requests must be submitted through the official HR portal or approved communication
channel.
Managers should approve or reject leave requests within two working days. HR will review policy
exceptions.
Attendance Regularization
Employees may regularize missed punches, incorrect login times, or approved work-from-home
attendance within three working days.
Regularization requests must include a clear reason an

unches, incorrect login times, or approved work-from-home
attendance within three working days.
Regularization requests must include a clear reason and manager confirmation.
